mapper.sql: v.violation_id, v.user_id, v.device_id, v.borrow_record_id, v.violation_start_date, v.violation_days, v.status, v.process_date, v.process_user_id, v.process_note, u.username, u.real_name, d.device_name, d.device_model, ba.end_date
mapper.insert: INSERT INTO violation (user_id, device_id, borrow_record_id, violation_start_date, violation_days, status) VALUES (#{userId}, #{deviceId}, #{borrowRecordId}, #{violationStartDate}, #{violationDays}, #{status})
mapper.select: SELECT FROM violation v JOIN `user` u ON v.user_id = u.user_id JOIN device d ON v.device_id = d.device_id JOIN borrow_apply ba ON v.borrow_record_id = ba.apply_id WHERE v.violation_id = #{violationId}

SELECT FROM violation v JOIN `user` u ON v.user_id = u.user_id JOIN device d ON v.device_id = d.device_id JOIN borrow_apply ba ON v.borrow_record_id = ba.apply_id WHERE v.borrow_record_id = #{borrowRecordId} LIMIT 1
mapper.update: UPDATE violation SET status = 'processed', process_date = #{processDate}, process_user_id = #{processUserId}, process_note = #{processNote} WHERE violation_id = #{violationId} AND status = 'untreated'
mapper.select: SELECT COUNT(1) FROM violation WHERE user_id = #{userId} AND status = 'untreated'

SELECT u.user_id AS id, CONCAT(u.real_name, '(', u.username, ')') AS name, COUNT(1) AS cnt FROM violation v JOIN `user` u ON v.user_id = u.user_id GROUP BY u.user_id, u.real_name, u.username ORDER BY cnt DESC, u.user_id ASC
SELECT d.device_id AS id, d.device_name AS name, COUNT(1) AS cnt FROM violation v JOIN device d ON v.device_id = d.device_id GROUP BY d.device_id, d.device_name ORDER BY cnt DESC, d.device_id ASC
